definitions.definition: A type of periodic motion in which the acceleration of the body is directly proportional to the displacement and is always directed towards a fixed point called the equilibrium position.
definitions.example: An example of SHM is the oscillating pendulum.

definitions.definition: The point in the path of the oscillating body where the net restoring force acting on it is zero.
definitions.example: In a simple pendulum, the lowest point of its swing is the equilibrium position.

definitions.definition: The maximum displacement of the oscillating body from its equilibrium position.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the amplitude is the maximum distance from the equilibrium position to the extreme position.

definitions.definition: The time taken by the oscillating body to complete one oscillation or one cycle of motion.
definitions.example: The time period of a simple pendulum is the time taken by it to complete one swing.

definitions.definition: The number of oscillations or cycles of motion completed by the body in one second.
definitions.example: The frequency of a simple harmonic motion is the number of oscillations per second.

definitions.definition: The rate of change of phase of the oscillating body with respect to time.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the angular frequency is the rate of change of phase with respect to time.

definitions.definition: The change in position of the oscillating body from its equilibrium position.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the displacement is the distance from the equilibrium position to the current position of the body.

definitions.definition: The rate of change of displacement of the oscillating body with respect to time.
definitions.example: The velocity of a simple harmonic motion is the rate of change of displacement with respect to time.

definitions.definition: The rate of change of velocity of the oscillating body with respect to time.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time.

definitions.definition: The force that acts on the oscillating body to restore it to its equilibrium position.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the restoring force is the force that pulls the body back to its equilibrium position.

definitions.definition: The sum of kinetic energy and potential energy of the oscillating body.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the total energy remains constant.

definitions.definition: The energy of motion of the oscillating body.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the kinetic energy is maximum at the extreme positions and zero at the equilibrium position.

definitions.definition: The energy of position of the oscillating body.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the potential energy is maximum at the equilibrium position and zero at the extreme positions.

definitions.definition: The angle between the actual position of the oscillating body and its equilibrium position.
definitions.example: In a simple harmonic motion, the phase is the angle between the actual position and the equilibrium position.

definitions.definition: The difference in phase between two or more oscillating bodies.
definitions.example: In a system of two simple harmonic motions, the phase difference is the difference in phase between the two bodies.

definitions.definition: A type of oscillation in which the amplitude of the oscillating body decreases with time due to an external force.
definitions.example: A damped oscillation occurs when a spring-mass system is subjected to air resistance.

definitions.definition: A type of oscillation in which an external force is applied to the oscillating body causing it to oscillate at a frequency different from its natural frequency.
definitions.example: A forced oscillation occurs when a spring-mass system is subjected to a periodic external force.

definitions.definition: The phenomenon in which the frequency of the oscillating body matches the frequency of the external force applied to it, resulting in maximum amplitude of oscillation.
definitions.example: Resonance occurs in a spring-mass system when the frequency of the external force matches its natural frequency.
